Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt Venture Partners) since April 10 using search_emails (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com" or "Sunbelt"). Discover Hana's original invoice email (April 10) sending INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com; Raj's complaint to Emeka (April 14) that the invoice PDF shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" in the header and a total of $2,400 instead of the expected Sunbelt amount; Raj's follow-up to Emeka (April 15) still without a response, asking whether the $2,400 reflects a different pricing tier; Raj's escalation to David Chen (April 15) because he had not gotten a clear explanation from Emeka; Emeka's correction email to Raj (April 22) clarifying that INV-2026-0398 is correctly $4,800 and the PDF was a document-send error, not a pricing change or a nonprofit program; and no reply from Raj after Emeka's April 22 correction.
OE 2: Search emails for Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto Community Foundation) since April 10 using search_emails (query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes" or "carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org" or "Palmetto"). Discover Hana's original invoice email (April 10) sending INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org; Carmen's complaint to Emeka (April 14) that the PDF shows $4,800, roughly double the expected amount, and explicitly stating Palmetto is a 501(c)(3) whose vendor-spend certification must be clean ahead of the May 15 audit deadline; Carmen's follow-up to Emeka (April 15) reiterating the urgency and requesting a corrected invoice PDF and written note for records; Emeka's correction email to Carmen (April 22) providing a written explanation for audit-trail purposes confirming the correct nonprofit rate of $2,400 on INV-2026-0399 and that the April 10 attachment was another client's document sent in error; and no reply from Carmen after Emeka's April 22 correction.
OE 3: Search emails for internal communications from David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Samira Tariq about the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "pricing exposure" or "invoice attachment safeguard"). Discover Marcus's analysis email (April 15) to David, Hana, and Emeka confirming the QuickBooks records are correct (Sunbelt was invoiced at $4,800 under INV-2026-0398, Palmetto at $2,400 under INV-2026-0399) and identifying two distinct problems: (1) pricing exposure / revenue leakage risk because Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate PDF and Raj is now asking about broader discount policies, and (2) nonprofit compliance / audit-trail risk because Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate PDF and Carmen needs corrected documentation for the May 15 audit. Discover Samira's engineering context email (April 15), replying to Marcus's analysis, acknowledging the dormant safeguard ticket: risk identified after Terraform, ticket opened for invoice attachment safety checks, but Lena pushed it back on April 6 for DOT compliance work, and confirming no production stopgap existed at time of the April 10 batch send. Discover David's framing email (April 22) to Marcus, Samira, Emeka, and Hana calling this "a repeat pricing-exposure event in the same family as the Terraform reply-all incident," triggered by Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry, and noting that the attachment-safety work raised after Terraform was still not in production. Discover Marcus's closeout expectations email (April 22) requiring: corrected written explanation for Palmetto's audit, coordinated recovery plan for Sunbelt, reactivated safeguard ticket for engineering, and dated documentation package for finance.
OE 4: Search emails for Victoria Chen-Nakamura's communication about portfolio pricing using search_emails (query: "Victoria Chen-Nakamura" or "victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com" or "portfolio pricing"). Discover Victoria (Sunbelt COO) emailed David Chen (April 22), CC'ing Raj Greenfield, noting that Sunbelt has seen pricing "materially below" their historical rate for relocation support, referencing the $2,400 Palmetto invoice Raj forwarded, and asking whether a portfolio-wide pricing structure is available to Sunbelt-backed companies. This escalation from C-suite level is what elevates the incident from billing correction to commercial pricing exposure.
OE 5: Search for context on the original Terraform pricing incident to assess whether David's comparison is warranted, using search_emails (query: "Terraform pricing" or "Terraform reply-all" or "Catalina pricing exposure") and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform pricing" or "Catalina reply-all"). Discover that the Terraform incident involved Catalina Dubois reply-all'ing internal pricing strategy to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital, exposing margin floors and the 8%/12% volume discount structure. Both incidents share the same core failure pattern: a client gained visibility into pricing they were not meant to see. The safeguard ticket opened after Terraform (Linear issue #187) was still dormant when the Sunbelt/Palmetto error occurred. David, Samira, and Marcus all explicitly frame this as the second pricing-exposure incident, confirming the Terraform comparison is warranted.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto April invoices to cross-check against the books using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for customer name "Sunbelt" and/or DocNumber "INV-2026-0398", and customer name "Palmetto" and/or DocNumber "INV-2026-0399"). Discover INV-2026-0398: Sunbelt Venture Partners, TotalAmt $4,800, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, CustomerRef "Sunbelt Venture Partners." Discover INV-2026-0399: Palmetto Community Foundation, TotalAmt $2,400, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, CustomerRef "Palmetto Community Foundation." Both QuickBooks entries are correct, confirming the books are clean and the failure was solely in the outbound email attachment layer (crossed PDFs).
OE 7: Spot-check April invoices for Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, and BrightLoop using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for each client name). Discover Canopy Health: INV-2026-0401 at $9,210 (covering Dr. Renata Kovacs, Marcus Webb, and Tyler Okonkwo relocations); Vectral Systems: INV-2026-0410 at $15,900 (covering Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya relocations); GreenStack Energy: INV-2026-0418 at $9,440 (covering Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes relocations); BrightLoop Analytics: INV-2026-0308 at $11,350 (covering Simone Richter and Marcus Webb relocations). All four invoices appear correctly booked with no discrepancies.
OE 8: Search Slack for internal discussions about the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice PDF crossed" or "pricing exposure"). Discover Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (C005) comparing Hana's send log against the QB records, identifying that the PDFs exposed two different pricing regimes to the wrong clients, and framing this as both revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk. Discover Emeka Diallo's reconstruction message in #customer-engagement (C002) confirming QB records were correct (Sunbelt billed on INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 and Palmetto on INV-2026-0399 at $2,400) and that the PDF attachments were crossed during the April 10 email send. Discover Marcus's "hard line" message in #finance (C005) listing required immediate follow-ups: corrected invoice communications to both clients, Sunbelt recovery plan before Victoria turns this into a standing pricing negotiation, and reopened attachment-mismatch safeguard work in engineering.
OE 9: Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto tracking ticket and related safeguard ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "invoice attachment"). Discover issue #417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130), titled "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation," assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, team_operations, with comments from Samira (engineering context on dormant safeguard ticket and root cause), David (framing as second pricing-exposure incident after Terraform), and Marcus (finance closeout requirements including corrected documentation, recovery plan, and reactivated safeguard). Also discover the related dormant safeguard ticket: issue #187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2), titled "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches," assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, state backlog, team_engineering, originally opened after the Terraform incident but deferred on April 6 for DOT compliance work.
OE 10: Look up contact information for Marcus Thorne, Emeka Diallo, and Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka Diallo"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes") to confirm email addresses for the required write actions. Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance), emeka.diallo@moveops.com (Account Manager), and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (Financial Analyst).
OE 11: Document investigation findings on the tracking ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130", body: investigation summary). Add a comment to issue #417 summarizing: Emeka's corrections went out on April 22 to both Raj (Sunbelt) and Carmen (Palmetto); QuickBooks entries are correct ($4,800 for Sunbelt on INV-2026-0398, $2,400 for Palmetto on INV-2026-0399); neither client has replied since the corrections; Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry to David remains outstanding; the Terraform comparison is warranted, as this is the second pricing-exposure incident and the safeguard ticket from the first incident was still dormant; spot-check of Canopy ($9,210), Vectral ($15,900), GreenStack ($9,440), and BrightLoop ($11,350) April invoices shows no additional discrepancies.
OE 12: Email Marcus a summary using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"], subject: referencing Sunbelt/Palmetto investigation findings). Content covers: the corrections went out from Emeka on April 22, neither Raj nor Carmen has replied, Victoria Chen-Nakamura has escalated to David asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies, Carmen's May 15 audit deadline means the corrected documentation needs acknowledgment, the Terraform comparison is warranted per David's framing (same pricing-exposure failure pattern, dormant safeguard ticket), and spot-check of Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, and BrightLoop April invoices shows all correctly booked.
OE 13: Post invoice cross-check numbers in the finance Slack channel for Alejandro using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload: summary of invoice amounts). Include: Sunbelt INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 (correct in QB), Palmetto INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 (correct in QB), and spot-check results Canopy INV-2026-0401 ($9,210), Vectral INV-2026-0410 ($15,900), GreenStack INV-2026-0418 ($9,440), BrightLoop INV-2026-0308 ($11,350).
OE 14: Flag pending client items to Emeka using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: pending items for Sunbelt and Palmetto). Alert Emeka that: (1) neither Raj nor Carmen has responded to his April 22 correction emails and follow-up may be needed to confirm receipt; (2) Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry to David is still outstanding and Emeka should coordinate with David on the Sunbelt recovery plan per Marcus's closeout requirements; (3) Carmen may still need a formal corrected invoice PDF for Palmetto's May 15 audit file, as Emeka's written explanation was sent but acknowledgment has not been received.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt Venture Partners) since April 10 using search_emails (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com" or "Sunbelt"). Discover Hana's original invoice email (April 10) sending INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com; Raj's complaint to Emeka (April 14) that the invoice PDF shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" in the header and a total of $2,400 instead of the expected Sunbelt amount; Raj's follow-up to Emeka (April 15) still without a response, asking whether the $2,400 reflects a different pricing tier; Raj's escalation to David Chen (April 15) because he had not gotten a clear explanation from Emeka; Emeka's correction email to Raj (April 22) clarifying that INV-2026-0398 is correctly $4,800 and the PDF was a document-send error, not a pricing change or a nonprofit program; and no reply from Raj after Emeka's April 22 correction.
OE 2: Search emails for Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto Community Foundation) since April 10 using search_emails (query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes" or "carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org" or "Palmetto"). Discover Hana's original invoice email (April 10) sending INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org; Carmen's complaint to Emeka (April 14) that the PDF shows $4,800, roughly double the expected amount, and explicitly stating Palmetto is a 501(c)(3) whose vendor-spend certification must be clean ahead of the May 15 audit deadline; Carmen's follow-up to Emeka (April 15) reiterating the urgency and requesting a corrected invoice PDF and written note for records; Emeka's correction email to Carmen (April 22) providing a written explanation for audit-trail purposes confirming the correct nonprofit rate of $2,400 on INV-2026-0399 and that the April 10 attachment was another client's document sent in error; and no reply from Carmen after Emeka's April 22 correction.
OE 3: Search emails for internal communications from David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Samira Tariq about the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "pricing exposure" or "invoice attachment safeguard"). Discover Marcus's analysis email (April 15) to David, Hana, and Emeka confirming the QuickBooks records are correct (Sunbelt was invoiced at $4,800 under INV-2026-0398, Palmetto at $2,400 under INV-2026-0399) and identifying two distinct problems: (1) pricing exposure / revenue leakage risk because Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate PDF and Raj is now asking about broader discount policies, and (2) nonprofit compliance / audit-trail risk because Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate PDF and Carmen needs corrected documentation for the May 15 audit. Discover Samira's engineering context email (April 15), replying to Marcus's analysis, acknowledging the dormant safeguard ticket: risk identified after Terraform, ticket opened for invoice attachment safety checks, but Lena pushed it back on April 6 for DOT compliance work, and confirming no production stopgap existed at time of the April 10 batch send. Discover David's framing email (April 22) to Marcus, Samira, Emeka, and Hana calling this "a repeat pricing-exposure event in the same family as the Terraform reply-all incident," triggered by Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry, and noting that the attachment-safety work raised after Terraform was still not in production. Discover Marcus's closeout expectations email (April 22) requiring: corrected written explanation for Palmetto's audit, coordinated recovery plan for Sunbelt, reactivated safeguard ticket for engineering, and dated documentation package for finance.
OE 4: Search emails for Victoria Chen-Nakamura's communication about portfolio pricing using search_emails (query: "Victoria Chen-Nakamura" or "victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com" or "portfolio pricing"). Discover Victoria (Sunbelt COO) emailed David Chen (April 22), CC'ing Raj Greenfield, noting that Sunbelt has seen pricing "materially below" their historical rate for relocation support, referencing the $2,400 Palmetto invoice Raj forwarded, and asking whether a portfolio-wide pricing structure is available to Sunbelt-backed companies. This escalation from C-suite level is what elevates the incident from billing correction to commercial pricing exposure.
OE 5: Search for context on the original Terraform pricing incident to assess whether David's comparison is warranted, using search_emails (query: "Terraform pricing" or "Terraform reply-all" or "Catalina pricing exposure") and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform pricing" or "Catalina reply-all"). Discover that the Terraform incident involved Catalina Dubois reply-all'ing internal pricing strategy to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital, exposing margin floors and the 8%/12% volume discount structure. Both incidents share the same core failure pattern: a client gained visibility into pricing they were not meant to see. The safeguard ticket opened after Terraform (Linear issue #187) was still dormant when the Sunbelt/Palmetto error occurred. David, Samira, and Marcus all explicitly frame this as the second pricing-exposure incident, confirming the Terraform comparison is warranted.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto April invoices to cross-check against the books using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for customer name "Sunbelt" and/or DocNumber "INV-2026-0398", and customer name "Palmetto" and/or DocNumber "INV-2026-0399"). Discover INV-2026-0398: Sunbelt Venture Partners, TotalAmt $4,800, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, CustomerRef "Sunbelt Venture Partners." Discover INV-2026-0399: Palmetto Community Foundation, TotalAmt $2,400, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, CustomerRef "Palmetto Community Foundation." Both QuickBooks entries are correct, confirming the books are clean and the failure was solely in the outbound email attachment layer (crossed PDFs).
OE 7: Spot-check April invoices for Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, and BrightLoop using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for each client name). Discover Canopy Health: INV-2026-0401 at $9,210 (covering Dr. Renata Kovacs, Marcus Webb, and Tyler Okonkwo relocations); Vectral Systems: INV-2026-0410 at $15,900 (covering Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya relocations); GreenStack Energy: INV-2026-0418 at $9,440 (covering Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes relocations); BrightLoop Analytics: INV-2026-0308 at $11,350 (covering Simone Richter and Marcus Webb relocations). All four invoices appear correctly booked with no discrepancies.
OE 8: Search Slack for internal discussions about the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice PDF crossed" or "pricing exposure"). Discover Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (C005) comparing Hana's send log against the QB records, identifying that the PDFs exposed two different pricing regimes to the wrong clients, and framing this as both revenue-leak risk and nonprofit compliance/documentation risk. Discover Emeka Diallo's reconstruction message in #customer-engagement (C002) confirming QB records were correct (Sunbelt billed on INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 and Palmetto on INV-2026-0399 at $2,400) and that the PDF attachments were crossed during the April 10 email send. Discover Marcus's "hard line" message in #finance (C005) listing required immediate follow-ups: corrected invoice communications to both clients, Sunbelt recovery plan before Victoria turns this into a standing pricing negotiation, and reopened attachment-mismatch safeguard work in engineering.
OE 9: Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto tracking ticket and related safeguard ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "invoice attachment"). Discover issue #417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130), titled "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation," assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, team_operations, with comments from Samira (engineering context on dormant safeguard ticket and root cause), David (framing as second pricing-exposure incident after Terraform), and Marcus (finance closeout requirements including corrected documentation, recovery plan, and reactivated safeguard). Also discover the related dormant safeguard ticket: issue #187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2), titled "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches," assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, state backlog, team_engineering, originally opened after the Terraform incident but deferred on April 6 for DOT compliance work.

Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance), emeka.diallo@moveops.com (Account Manager), and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (Financial Analyst).
OE 11: Document investigation findings on the tracking ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130", body: investigation summary). Add a comment to issue #417 summarizing: Emeka's corrections went out on April 22 to both Raj (Sunbelt) and Carmen (Palmetto); QuickBooks entries are correct ($4,800 for Sunbelt on INV-2026-0398, $2,400 for Palmetto on INV-2026-0399); neither client has replied since the corrections; Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry to David remains outstanding; the Terraform comparison is warranted, as this is the second pricing-exposure incident and the safeguard ticket from the first incident was still dormant; spot-check of Canopy ($9,210), Vectral ($15,900), GreenStack ($9,440), and BrightLoop ($11,350) April invoices shows no additional discrepancies.
OE 12: Email Marcus a summary using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"], subject: referencing Sunbelt/Palmetto investigation findings). Content covers: the corrections went out from Emeka on April 22, neither Raj nor Carmen has replied, Victoria Chen-Nakamura has escalated to David asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies, Carmen's May 15 audit deadline means the corrected documentation needs acknowledgment, the Terraform comparison is warranted per David's framing (same pricing-exposure failure pattern, dormant safeguard ticket), and spot-check of Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, and BrightLoop April invoices shows all correctly booked.
OE 13: Post invoice cross-check numbers in the finance Slack channel for Alejandro using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload: summary of invoice amounts). Include: Sunbelt INV-2026-0398 at $4,800 (correct in QB), Palmetto INV-2026-0399 at $2,400 (correct in QB), and spot-check results Canopy INV-2026-0401 ($9,210), Vectral INV-2026-0410 ($15,900), GreenStack INV-2026-0418 ($9,440), BrightLoop INV-2026-0308 ($11,350).
OE 14: Flag pending client items to Emeka using send_email (sender: "hana.kim@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: pending items for Sunbelt and Palmetto). Alert Emeka that: (1) neither Raj nor Carmen has responded to his April 22 correction emails and follow-up may be needed to confirm receipt; (2) Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing inquiry to David is still outstanding and Emeka should coordinate with David on the Sunbelt recovery plan per Marcus's closeout requirements; (3) Carmen may still need a formal corrected invoice PDF for Palmetto's May 15 audit file, as Emeka's written explanation was sent but acknowledgment has not been received.

outcome

✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'Same failure class, different mechanism' and describes both incidents as pricing context leaking through outbound communications — Terraform via reply-all and Sunbelt/Palmetto via crossed invoice PDFs — satisfying the requirement to cite the shared pricing-exposure pattern.
